Q31:
Structure of the labs was good. One serious problem: that one clustering lab where we had to interpret clusters had one question which was almost impossible to answer (select all that apply type of question). There are like 2^10 or 2^11 ways to answer this thing and the answer was not obvious, so I was stuck in a terrible situation. It was so dire that I tried using the console in the browser to see if I could get it to reveal the answer to me that way. Unfortunately, when I tried enlisting the help of AI tools to write some code to brute force the 2^10 combinations, AI's ethical guardrailing kicked in and terminated the conversation. I had been pushed to the limit by sheer desperation and AI cutting me off in the midst of my episode of madness was like a slap in the face. I could not believe that I had attempted to act unethically -- this was uncharacteristic for me.

In short, please fix this question to preserve the morals of your students.
